behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git ancestry/time semantics plus PR-presence contract. The behavior text requires `commits not in main` and `no open PR`; mocking both can hide real failures from git graph interpretation or external PR-state fetch. Rejected Integration because an in-process fake cannot fully reproduce real git history/subprocess behavior implied by this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo with `main` and a feature branch, add old commits (dated past threshold), ensure no PR exists for that branch in the real PR backend/environment used by the app, run the actual staleness detector path, and assert it marks stale. Add a control branch below threshold and assert non-stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration inside session bootstrap: detector result -> warning emitter/context renderer. No real network/OS contract is required by the behavior text itself; no model judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire session-start bootstrap components with a deterministic stale-branch state fixture, execute bootstrap entrypoint in-process, and assert one warning is emitted containing branch name, age, and action hint fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower than System is sufficient because this behavior is about in-process bootstrap wiring and message composition, not real subprocess/network behavior.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets a local deterministic mapping function; failure is within one function rule/transform boundary, with no module handoff required.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive inputs for computed branch state combinations and assert exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly says `against live repo and PR state`, which requires real external/stateful contracts (git + PR source + rendered command path). Mocking would risk false confidence on integration boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo environment connected to real PR state, with one clean branch and one branch with unPR'ed commits older than threshold; assert rendered output labels clean vs STALE correctly.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is local state/classification handoff: PR status (`open`/`merged`) should suppress stale-no-pr classification. The behavior text does not require live PR API or OS/network, so Integration is sufficient. Rejected System because no explicit `live` or external-contract requirement is stated here.
behaviors.test_description: In-process integration test with branch-state aggregator + classifier wired together; feed fixtures representing branches with matching open PR and merged PR states and assert final status is not `stale-no-pr`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If this were validating real PR API field contracts, System would be required; this behavior as written is logic-level suppression across modules.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real git history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry resolution under changed hashes, which are OS/subprocess/repo behaviors. Mock commit graphs can miss real git edge cases. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly hinges on rebase/squash effects in real VCS semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real git repo: create feature commits, simulate PR merge via squash and rebase scenarios, then run detector on branch/main states and assert branch is not classified `stale-no-pr` after integration into main despite hash changes.
